description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service and default staging variables. Define three stages: build, deploy, and stop_review. In the build stage add a build job that runs: export IMAGE=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:${CI_BUILD_TAG:="v"$CI_BUILD_REF}; docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY; docker build -t builder -f Dockerfile-dev .; docker run --rm -v $PWD:/application builder bash -c 'SECRET_KEY_BASE=onetwo RAILS_ENV=production bundle exec rake assets:precompile'; docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:v$CI_BUILD_REF -f Dockerfile .; docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:v$CI_BUILD_REF; docker rmi -f $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:v$CI_BUILD_REF. In the deploy stage add three jobs: deploy_production runs only on the master branch using thehumaneffort/kubectl-and-envsubst:v1.0.1 image, sets environment production, exports NAME, IMAGE, VIRTUAL_HOST and applies the rendered Kubernetes manifest with DOLLAR='$' envsubst < deploy/kube.tmpl.yaml | bin/kubectl apply -f -; deploy_review runs on all branches except master and develop, also uses the same image, creates a review environment with on_stop stop_review, exports NAME, IMAGE, VIRTUAL_HOST, BRANCH_DB, patches the deployment to zero replicas, dumps and restores the staging PostgreSQL database into a branch‑specific DB via kubectl exec, then applies the manifest with envsubst; deploy_staging runs only on develop, uses the same image, sets staging environment variables and applies the manifest similarly. Finally, add a manual stop_review job in the stop_review stage that uses the same kubectl image to export NAME and delete the review deployment and service with bin/kubectl delete deployment $NAME and bin/kubectl delete service $NAME.
